new_classes.label_en: Wine with Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) in containers > 2 L and ≤ 10 L (subheading 2204 22 26)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter Ursprungsbezeichnung (g.U.) in Behältnissen > 2 L und ≤ 10 L (Unterposition 2204 22 26)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers of more than 2 litres and up to and including 10 litres, classified under CN subheading 2204 22 26.

coverage_explanation: The key distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 22 26 are: (1) wine of fresh grapes (captured by subclassing WineOfFreshGrapes2204), (2) bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) as per Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 (captured via the ProtectedDesignationOfOriginOrGeographicalIndicationQuality restriction — note this quality covers both PDO and PGI; a dedicated PDO-only quality class would improve precision but is not in the TBox), and (3) presented in containers of more than 2 L and up to and including 10 L (captured via the LargeContainer part restriction and the liquidVolumeInLitres range restrictions). The coverage gap (−0.18) arises from: (a) the PDO quality class in the TBox conflates PDO and PGI rather than distinguishing them strictly; Additional Note 6(a) is referenced textually but its detailed content (definition of PDO wine criteria) is not further decomposed in the ontology; (b) sibling subheadings 22042222–22042278 covering various PDO wine sub-types (different sugar content, colour, etc.) are not differentiated here as no further sub-criteria are provided in the supplied legal note text for this specific 8-digit code.
